issue: Warnings in pipelineHealthWarnings (e.g. missing_high_priority_org) were pushed into the 'errors' array, causing process.exitCode = 1 despite being non-fatal warnings
root_cause: Line 1227: `if (pipelineHealthWarnings.length) errors.push(...)` — warnings and hard failures shared the same array. The `main()` function checked `report.errors.length` to set exit code 1, so any warning triggered a non-zero exit.

fix.change_3: Added `warnings` to the returned report object alongside `errors` (line 1285)
fix.effect: `pipelineHealthWarnings` are now reported in the `warnings` array. Only hard errors (in `errors`) cause `exitCode = 1`. Warnings are visible in output but don't fail the pipeline.

impact.before: npm run jobs:validate-public-data would exit code 1 whenever a high-priority org like 'protect democracy' was missing from the public board, or similar non-fatal warnings existed. This blocked subsequent workflow steps (guard blocked sources, commit, etc.)
impact.after: Only hard validation failures and pipeline health failures cause exit code 1. Non-fatal pipeline health warnings are reported in JSON output but don't fail the step.

test: `npm run jobs:validate-public-data` will now succeed (exit 0) even when warnings are present, as long as no hard validation failures exist
